attempts.strategy: S6 step (d-body) — peel (-1)^i • Pi.lift f_i per-summand using a 4-lemma simp set: Pi.lift_π_apply collapses (Pi.π j).hom ((Pi.lift f).hom x) to (f j).hom x; ModuleCat.hom_smul + LinearMap.smul_apply peel the scalar (-1)^i through the .hom projection; ConcreteCategory.comp_apply normalises the ≫-composition inside f j.

attempts.goal_before: Per-summand (post-iter-090-(d-entry), for fixed i): (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (((-1)^i • Pi.lift (fun a => Pi.π Z₁ (a ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom) ≫ (toModuleKPresheaf C).map φ_i.op)).hom ((piIsoPi Z₁).inv (r • y))) = r • (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (((-1)^i • Pi.lift (fun a => Pi.π Z₁ (a ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom) ≫ (toModuleKPresheaf C).map φ_i.op)).hom ((piIsoPi Z₁).inv y))
attempts.goal_after: Expected (unverified): (-1)^i • (Pi.π Z₁ (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom) ≫ (toModuleKPresheaf C).map φ_i.op).hom ((piIsoPi Z₁).inv (r • y)) = r • (-1)^i • (Pi.π Z₁ (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom) ≫ (toModuleKPresheaf C).map φ_i.op).hom ((piIsoPi Z₁).inv y)
attempts.insight: Combined simp set covers four orthogonal reductions: lift-projection collapse, composition unfold, ModuleCat smul peel, LinearMap smul peel. Risk: ModuleCat.hom_smul is the natural-scalar variant; integer (-1)^i may require the zsmul variant ModuleCat.hom_zsmul + LinearMap.zsmul_apply. Fallback documented but not applied since the directive mandates the bare primary form.

attempts.strategy: S6 step (e) — surface inner R-action via Pi.smul_apply: (r • y) (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom) = r • y (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom). perI₁ is in scope from the theorem's letI block at L472-L481.
attempts.goal_before: Expected (unverified, after step (d-body)): (-1)^i • (Pi.π Z₁ _ ≫ (toModuleKPresheaf C).map φ_i.op).hom ((piIsoPi Z₁).inv (r • y)) = r • (-1)^i • (Pi.π Z₁ _ ≫ (toModuleKPresheaf C).map φ_i.op).hom ((piIsoPi Z₁).inv y)
attempts.goal_after: Expected (unverified): now the inner (r • y)(_) has been peeled to r • y(_), making the RingHom-map_mul split in step (f) applicable.
attempts.insight: Pi.smul_apply requires the term form (r • y)(a) — if iter-090's (piIsoPi Z₁).inv wrapping wasn't simplified by (d-body)'s simp set, the (r • y) may still be wrapped inside .inv. Fallback letI := perI₁ prefix documented but not applied. The iter-090 risk-list called out this exact synthesis risk.

attempts.strategy: S6 step (f) — split ((toModuleKPresheaf C).map φ_i.op).hom over the restricted product r * y_at_{j∘δ_i}. The .hom of a CommRingCat-valued presheaf map is a RingHom, so map_mul fires.
attempts.goal_before: Expected (unverified, after step (e)): (-1)^i • (Pi.π Z₁ _ ≫ (toModuleKPresheaf C).map φ_i.op).hom (r' * y_at_{j∘δ_i}) = r • (-1)^i • (Pi.π Z₁ _ ≫ (toModuleKPresheaf C).map φ_i.op).hom y_at_{j∘δ_i}
attempts.goal_after: Expected (unverified): (-1)^i • ((presheaf.map φ_i.op).hom r' * (presheaf.map φ_i.op).hom y_at_{j∘δ_i}) = r • (-1)^i • (presheaf.map φ_i.op).hom y_at_{j∘δ_i}

attempts.strategy: S6 closure — after (g)'s collapse and the RHS's r •-action reducing to the same direct restriction-multiplication shape via perI₂, both sides match definitionally with ≤-witness proof equality, so rfl closes.
attempts.goal_before: Expected (unverified, after step (g)): LHS and RHS both reduced to (-1)^i • [(presheaf.map _.op).hom r * (presheaf.map _.op).hom (y(_))] with only differences being proof terms of V_j ≤ U, equal by proof irrelevance.
attempts.insight: rfl closes via definitional equality + proof irrelevance on the ≤-witnesses. If the residue is non-definitional (e.g., a remaining ConcreteCategory.comp_apply unfolding gap), fallback is simp only [ConcreteCategory.comp_apply] then ring or congr 1 <;> exact proof_irrel _ _. The bare rfl is the directive's mandated primary form.
